title: 1978-10-10_SECTION_016_285.txt
raw_text: VALIS) and Christ/Valis/YHWH invades it. This is why realm #3 is exploded through thousands of years of our time. Realm #3 is the real world. So a message there that is intact is exploded here through thousands of our dilated time years. Like the meta-morpheme in TEARS -- one morpheme in a message. I see. A message is transmitted from the real world (realm #3) to our world. In realm #3 the real world it takes a minute. Here, it spans 2000 years. Look at the communications problem. Whole civilizations rise and fall while they --in realm #3-- transmit one message to us. The message is exploded over the span of two thousand of our years so although they're aware of us, how can they communicate with us? This is dealt with in Varley's story. But incorrectly! They'd have to speed up a message so that the total message was transmitted in a billionth of a second by their real elapsed time. Maybe that's how the AI voice works. All its messages to me were transmitted in a single billionth of a second and arrive spread out over years. Now I'm getting fanciful. But still -- They would try to ride and cook our communications media in an effort to communicate with us, reach us. And of course they'd be communicating in Greek, Latin, Hebrew. Hebrew! Maybe we're real small; space there is enormous compared to ours. They would put a probe of some kind through. Valis. Perturb our reality field. {folder 01 - 058} 10-20-80 10 I'm going to hazard a wild guess about the vast amounts of space I experienced in 3-74. It's a clue, a verification. If we in our world (realm #4) experience no real elapsed time, only spurious time, well, space and time are inter-related. "Here time turns into space." We lose time (we have no real time) so instead we have a conversion to space, to augmented space. Or something like that. Time for us -- that's it. "He" deprived us of real time by converting it into space -- which I then saw when I ceased to be blind. Much space and no time. (I can't get this quite right.) No; it works like this. While we're in a deluded, blinded, asleep state, space is converted into a high flux rate of spurious time, so space shrinks. But if you wake up, the hyperflux is abolished, and as a result space for you is no longer converted into hyper but spurious time; that's it. Since no actual real elapsed time passes, you'd see all space. But when we're deluded, our hyperflux has to draw on space, our space, not actual space -- no; on real space. That's how our hyperflux is created: by a conversion of space to time. Then if one of us wakes up. "time" (hyperflux) is transformed back to space, which it really is, and that person experiences vast volumes of space. Kozyrev's theory of time "as energy poured into a material system." View space and time as space-time and then view it as energy poured into a material system. If hyperflux is induced, space will suffer. But this is not real time; nonetheless it is created out of space, because there has to be a constant total energy amount in the system, in one form or another, as space or time. On the other hand (to go back to what I first said) if no time is elapsing, then there is in fact unusually large volumes of space, but we are blinded to this. However, once we wake up and see that the last "2000 years" is irreal, irreal time, then we see the space there instead; it perceptually converts back. One of these two explanations is correct, depending on whether the concept "no time" is right or "irreal time" which is hyperflux. Based on what the AI voice said, "no time has passed," so in actuality our normal amount of time has been converted into space, precisely as I say in VALIS: "Here time becomes space." This is the real state of things, in contrast to what we subjectively experience. So our realm #4 is a nontime, vast-space sub-universe. To realm #3 we must appear gaseous and speeded-up. {folder 01 - 059} 10-21-80 1 If they had a common essence, space and time didn't really exist. Was this what I realized? Not just that USA 1974 and Rome AD 4 were accidents --aspects-- of a common essence, but, this being so, my mind leaped instantly to the realization that in a certain real sense time and space did not actually exist -- with all that this implies. So I suddenly was no longer ordering reality by the basic ontological categories of space and time. How then was I ordering it? Morphologically: by morphological arrangement. This is another way of seeing reality entirely, and this is Plato's Form world. So even if what happened (my seeing USA 1974 and Rome AD 45 superimposed, comingled, unified) actually indicated something else (for example that we are in a spurious world, an "interpolation by Satan in God's creation") I nonetheless performed the Platonist leap. And this leap was due, as Plato says, to anamnesis; therefore the functions performed by my brain were what he describes and why. The cause was the right cause and the meta-abstraction was the right meta-abstraction ... and the results were what Plotinus describes. So pragmatically I underwent a Platonist-Neo-Platonist leap from this space-time realm to the realm of Forms which is as Plotinus says the realm of Nous. So how in a practical sense does my experience differ from what Plotinus and Plato call for? In no sense at all. The results in all degrees and all areas and aspects were as Plotinus depicts. One might even argue that the Christian element, real as it was, got subsumed by a Neo-Platonist ultra brain-function. I saw morphological arrangement (Plato's Form world). I saw it due to a comprehension, an insight, based on recollection of a prenatal existence. Well, then I should be satisfied. Plato is right: if you experience anamnesis --the sudden recollection of prenatal memory due to a certain event or stimulus-- your brain will make the leap to morphological arrangement rather than spatiotemporal arrangement. Plato is vindicated. He says, "If you remember your former life --due to a certain set of circumstances, something that triggers off your buried, latent or blocked memory-- you will grasp the fact of the reality of universals, of the Form world." I did exactly this, in a single flash (Blitz). So Plato is proved right. To me this meta brain-function is of more interest than the Christian content, since I am concerned with species evolution. {folder 01 - 060} 10-21-80 2 What is particularly important is that once your brain begins to perceive reality in terms of morphological arrangement rather than by the categories of space and time you see whole realms that you never knew existed before. This, too, vindicates Plato and Plotinus in their Orphic view that the soul fell, is incarnated again and again, and; when this morphological ordering sets in, rises back up the axis along which it originally fell. Now, the Christian-Gnostic interpretation is that Thomas in Rome AD 45 was not a former life on my part but is the real world now; but this is not what my brain inferred there at that moment in 2-74 when I saw the golden fish sign. I did not infer that USA 1974 does not exist; I inferred that the two spatiotemporal continua (USA 1974 and Rome AD45) were essentially one and the same. I did not find myself in Rome AD 45 instead of USA 1974; I found myself in both at once, in what I have always called a "superimposition" or "double exposure." It was not a matter of A or not-A; it was a matter of both. I was unwilling then and I am unwilling now to go the route of Aristotle and say that one of the two has to be real (A) and one false (not-A), but that both together cannot exist; i.e. it is either Rome AD 45 or it is USA 1974 but it cannot be one reality made up of Rome AD45 and USA 1974 --and yet the latter is exactly the case. Which, as I say, caused me to infer a common essence --one substantia-- with two aspects. What most impressed me was not so-to-speak the attempt at superimposition but the fact that it could occur without conflict between the two spatiotemporal continua. It wasn't like a movie dissolve and it wasn't like a conflicting double exposure; the two continua blended perfectly, effortlessly, into a single reality. That is what impressed me. No conflict, no mutually exclusive competition. I simply was confronted by one integrated reality made up of both continua. And there could be only one explanation. USA 1974 wasn't really Rome AD 45 (as I've tended to think all these years) any more than Rome AD 45 was really USA 1974. This is where I made my conscious error; this is where the right extrapolation failed at least on a conscious, verbal basis; but on an unconscious intuitive level my brain understood the meaning of what it saw: that space and time are not real, and the actually arrangement of reality is morphological and not spatiotemporal. This is the Platonist metaphysics per se. This is the heart of what Plato taught about the reality of the Forms. {folder 01 - 061} 10-21-80 3 As a matter of fact it is quite possible that my failure to understand, for six and a half years, that both continua were equally real may be due to an unconscious A or not-A either-or Aristotelian mode of thinking on my part; I saw USA 1974 and I saw Rome AD 4S and my ossified brain components said, "One must be real and the other not real," but another part of my brain, less rigid, less in the grip of conventional modes of logic, realized, "Both are real. Both equally exist. Further, both are one. They are modes, aspects or accidents --whatever you want to call it-- of a single essence. This is the only way you can explain syntonic superimposition. Now, if the superimposition had been competitive --that would have required another theory entirely. I guess in that case I could have inferred something about time and space not being real, but I could not infer that the spatiotemporal continua were aspects of a common substratum a common essence. There would simply have confronted me a breakdown of the ordering categories of space and time, with no inference to be drawn by a tautalogy: space and time as ordering categories broke down and I saw two spaces and two times superimposed ... what we normally mean by a double exposure. But, see, one harmonious reality was formed by the two; so the great inference of accidents --two sets of accidents-- to a common essence took place in my brain, whereupon the realization came to my brain that space and time were not the best or the only way of ordering reality but that it could better be ordered morphologically. And then I found in TEARS where the USA, the USSR, Nazi Germany and ancient Rome and melded into one society -- oh yes; to that add futuristic elements. So we have five societies, five space-time continua as a single matrix. The strangest most eerie thought just struck me. If USA 1974 and Rome AD 45 are two spatiotemporal aspects of a common essence they as aspects if superimposed would clash, not blend -- this despite the common essence; I don't know why I know this but I do. But if it's really Rome AD 45 then they would blend, because the percipient would see that USA 1974 is Rome AD 45. What I have been calling a superimposition is more like
